channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Global Tensions Rise as US and Iran Clash Over Hormuz, Volkswagen Cuts Jobs, and Rand Weakens
item.description: The article highlights several critical global developments. The United States has intensified military strikes on Iran over the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump imposing a 20% fee for ships pas...

item.title: Market Participants Boost Wagers on Interest Rate Rises
item.description: The article discusses how financial market participants are increasing their bets on potential interest rate hikes by central banks, particularly the Federal Reserve. Traders and investors are adjusti...

item.title: Ethiopia's Axum Church Declaration Sparks Constitutional Conflict and Religious Dispute
item.description: The Tigray Orthodox Tewahedo Church Synod's declaration of Axum as 'Holy Axum of St. Mary of Zion' has ignited a constitutional conflict with Ethiopia's government. This move, which mandates alcohol a...

item.title: Arrest of Karachi Rangers Camp Attack Mastermind and Links to Afghan Training
item.description: The police have arrested the mastermind behind the deadly attack on a Rangers camp in Karachi, which resulted in three security personnel killed and four injured. The attackers were linked to an India...

item.title: Kew Council Challenges Approval of Controversial Supermarket Site Development
item.description: The Boroondara Council has launched a legal challenge against Planning Minister Sonya Kilkenny and developer Orchard Piper over the approval of a $520 million residential and retail project on the for...

item.title: Mother-in-Law's Gift Sparks Debate Over Family Involvement in Home Purchase
item.description: The article discusses a family conflict involving a mother-in-law who gifted a significant sum toward a down payment for her son's new home. The couple, who currently live together in a condo purchase...

item.title: Survey Indicates Possible Run-off in 2027 Kenyan Presidential Election
item.description: A new Infotrak poll highlights that a presidential election held today would likely result in a run-off, as no candidate secured the required 50%+1 majority. Under Kenya's constitution, a winner must ...

item.title: Economists Urge Immediate Action to Address AI's Economic Impact and Job Risks
item.description: A coalition of over 200 economists, AI researchers, and tech executives has issued an open letter urging immediate action to manage the transformative potential of artificial intelligence. The stateme...

item.title: Kenya Seizes Sh4.9 Billion in Smuggled Minerals
item.description: Kenyan authorities intercepted minerals valued at approximately 4.9 billion shillings being smuggled out of the country, marking a significant crackdown on illegal mineral trade in East Africa. The se...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Sunetra Pawar's NCP Presidency
item.description: A former national secretary of the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) has filed a legal challenge against the election of Sunetra Pawar as the party's national president. The legal notice, sent on July ...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of 'Suez': Annabella's Journey in Hollywood's Golden Era
item.description: The article explores the legendary production of 20th Century Fox’s romantic historical epic *Suez* (1938), focusing on French actress Annabella’s pivotal role as Toni Pellerin. Her performance, which...

item.title: Understanding environmental decline through shifting baselines
item.description: Tom Eaton's column explores how human short-term memory and shifting baseline syndrome obscure our perception of ecological collapse. The article argues that generations fail to recognize the scale of...

item.title: NDRRMC Reports 23 Fatalities and 18 Missing Due to Inday and Habagat
item.description: Ang National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) ay nagsabi na ang bilang ng mga reportado na patay dahil sa enhanced Southwest Monsoon (Habagat) at Tropical Cyclone Inday ay umabo...

item.title: Wildlife Rescuers Address Bird Survival Challenges Amid Pakistan's Rising Temperatures
item.description: Pakistan's wildlife rescuers are working tirelessly to save birds from the deadly effects of rising temperatures and climate change. As Islamabad experiences record-breaking heatwaves, with summer tem...

item.title: Toronto Police Identify Two Victims in Targeted Shooting at Street Festival
item.description: Toronto police have identified two men killed in a targeted shooting at a crowded street festival, highlighting the tragic incident that disrupted the event. The victims, Shaquan Quashie (25) and Cesa...

item.title: Spacex Shares Face Pressure Amid Market Volatility
item.description: The stock price of SpaceX, a leading aerospace manufacturer and space transportation company, has recently experienced significant fluctuations, with shares nearing new lows. This development comes am...

item.title: Kaduna's Revenue Surge: Tech, Tax Reforms, and Expanded Tax Base Drive Growth
item.description: Kaduna State's Internally Generated Revenue (IGR) has surged to over N86 billion in 2025, with projections of hitting N120 billion by year-end. Governor Uba Sani's administration attributes this growt...

item.title: Ukraine-Russia Conflict Escalates as Kyiv Targets Russian Vessels; Zelensky Urges Air Defences

item.title: YAP1 Variability Drives Stable Tissue Patterns During Regeneration
item.description: Researchers from the Friedrich Miescher Institute for Biomedical Research and collaborators discovered that regenerating intestinal tissue transforms minor initial differences between cells into stabl...

item.title: Reviving a 200-Year-Old Physics Experiment to Advance Optical Computing
item.description: Scientists at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore have discovered a groundbreaking method to create optical skyrmions using a 200-year-old optical phenomenon known as the Poisson spot. This ...
